segments.text: [Music]
hi i'm anne campbell and today i'm going
to talk about some of the key features
in sonar cube 9.6
we've added a lot of new rules in this
version in some interesting categories
and i'm going to start with those
so first
ta-da kubernetes rules yes
infrastructure as code is code you've
been telling us for a while that you
want to analyze it and we've heard you
so we started by adding one new code
smell rule and six new security hotspot
rules
here's an example security hotspot being
raised on the use of privileged true
as with every security hotspot with
these kubernetes security hotspots we're
going to help you understand what the
risk is we're going to help you assess
whether or not in your particular
situation you are at risk and if you
think you are we'll give you some advice
on how to fix it
staying in the cloud but moving to java
we've added some rules to help you write
java aws lambdas the key themes here are
aws best practices and the use of the
aws cdk
so here's an example issue we've got one
lambda calling another an issue is
raised on that because the first lambda
could time out while it's waiting for
the second lambda to come back that
should be happening asynchronously
instead
staying in the cloud but moving on to c
sharp we've added rules for azure
functions this time
six new code smell rules with the themes
of resource management error handling
and entity interface design help you
write better azure functions
here are some examples in sample code so
first we've got
error handling telling us to use a try
catch block in the body of the function
as well as some resource management
issues
staying with c sharp but moving out of a
cloud we've added support for
deconstruction this time so now you see
that we can properly raise issues on the
deconstruction syntax
wrapping up rules and issues let's talk
about analysis itself
faster analysis is a key theme for us
this year because we understand that
it's a key theme for you we've been
working hard this year to make analysis
faster and this time we've added
something that folks have been waiting a
long time for faster java pr analysis
we did that by adding an analysis cache
at the server side what this means is
that it's independent of analysis
location so as your job moves from build
agent to build agent this is still going
to work now we just finished putting a
bow on this like right before release so
we haven't had time to run full
statistics on it yet but in one of the
test projects we were looking at we saw
pr analysis drop from 120 seconds excuse
me 160 seconds to 20 seconds and we
think that's going to scale out to your
projects as well
now we just proved out the technology
but it's not java specific we proved it
out with java but we expect to
push it out to more languages very
quickly
moving on from that we've also improved
tein analysis in this version
by recognizing custom validators now
previously when you used a validator
provided by a library we understood that
and then didn't raise an issue on
subsequent use of the user-provided data
but if you wrote your own validator we
didn't know what that was and so we were
raising some false positive issues on
your code so now we're going to
recognize the validators that you write
as well
when they follow particular patterns so
if it returns a boolean it follows a
naming convention that we understand
and it calls a known validator method
such as a pattern matcher or a regular
expression then we're going to recognize
that as a custom validator and
understand that if the user provided
data gets through that it's okay to use
we've also updated our language version
understanding so we now parse properly
scala 213 8 and 312
ruby 3.1 kotlin 1.7 and apex 54.12
for go in addition to the parsing we've
also updated the rules to properly deal
with go 1.18
moving on from there let's talk about
security reports in this version we've
added a pci dss report this is about
credit card processing and the industry
has 12 high level guidelines that break
down into 240 specific rules
now most of those are not statically
checkable so this report is only about
the things that we can check through
analysis but we've added it for both
versions 4.0 and 3.2 of the standard you
see both of those versions in the
interface we've also added the 4.0
report to the pdf
sticking with the theme of security but
moving to the security of analysis in
the sonarcube instance let's talk about
analysis tokens
so in 9.5 we added the ability to select
a token type so you can narrow
what can be done with your analysis
tokens
in 9.6 we go further to give you the
ability to expire your tokens so you
still have the ability to issue a token
with no expiration but you can also
limit the life of your tokens
so
for one year maybe now that's what i can
do as a user as a global admin i have
the ability to limit the token analysis
excuse me the token lifespan that users
can choose so let's narrow this down to
30 days i'm going to save that
and now when the user comes back in
which i'm going to simulate by reloading
you see that i only have the one option
any new token i issue can only last for
30 days
coming back to this global admin
interface i do want to show you
something else related to the security
of the instance
which is that you can now sign your saml
requests
and you can use assertion encryption
with your saml requests i think a lot of
people will be happy to see that added
as well
now let's talk about the interface
itself so here i am on the projects page
your antenna may have quivered when i
loaded this page because it does look
subtly different
you'll see that the contrast has
strengthened here in the facets on the
left we've also added some explicit
wording where we were in places just
using the icons to convey meaning
we did this as a way to improve the
accessibility of this page
this is something that we're going to be
working on through the end of this year
and into the next year with a goal of
making our entire interface accessible
to all of our users where sadly today
it's not always doing great at that
we've started on the projects page and
in a few other spots in the interface
and you should see more changes going
forward
also on the interface
in 9.5
we gave you an updated issue
presentation to help you focus on the
issue at hand we further iterated on
that in 9.6
to move all of the metadata about the
issue here into the header so i've still
got all of the same information i had
before
but this the type the severity the
status and so on are pulled up out of
the way of the issue and code so now i'm
focused purely on
where is the problem and what should i
do to fix it and of course i still have
access easy access to the rule
description
speaking of rule descriptions forte
analysis rules we've done significant
work this time to expand those rule
descriptions to help you better
understand from them and learn from them
what's going on security wise with your
project to give you the larger
understanding so that you can fix not
just the issue at hand but maybe prevent
introducing other issues in the future
so we've got the why is this an issue
section that you're used to seeing this
is the basic description data we've
added to that some
specific information on how to fix
issues
based based on the framework that you're
using so we start here with jsp if i'm
using jsp and i have an issue there i've
got specific jsp related information
on how to go about fixing that issue but
it what if i'm using a servlet well
we've got servlet specific information
spring specific information time leaf
and etc
when i have an actual issue raised the
correct
information for the framework i'm using
is going to be pre-selected so i don't
even have to do the work of clicking
around here
it's already ready for me when i come in
beyond that we've added some basic
information on
basically other reading so if you want
to go even further if you want to delve
deeper into the topic the data is here
for you to learn and we've also added
some fundamentals so we're calling them
security principles to help you really
beef up your security chops
if you're ready to do that we've put the
information here for you
so you can be a better stronger more
secure coder
and that's what i wanted to show you
this time
and i'll see you next time thanks for
listening

security hotspots we're going to help you understand what the risk is we're going to help you assess whether or not in your particular situation you are at risk and if you think you are we'll give you happening asynchronously instead staying in the cloud but moving on to c sharp we've added rules for azure functions this time six new code smell rules with the themes of resource management error handling and entity interface design help you write better azure functions here are some examples in sample code so first we've got error handling telling us to use a try catch block in the body of the function as well as some resource management issues staying with c sharp but moving out of a cloud we've added support for deconstruction this time so now you see that we can properly raise issues on the deconstruction syntax wrapping up rules and issues let's talk about analysis itself faster analysis is a key theme for us this year because we understand that it's a key theme for you we've the server side what this means is that it's independent of analysis location so as your job moves from build agent to build agent this is still going to work now we just finished putting a bow on to 20 seconds and we think that's going to scale out to your projects as well now we just proved out the technology but it's not java specific we proved it out with java but we expect to push it out
